title: koeleria kurtzii
intro: Koeleria kurtzii, also known as Kurtz's hair grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of China and India, valued for its medicinal properties. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Koeleria kurtzii are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
